text: wore the same shirt to work 3 days in a row and nobody noticed until day 4 when karen from hr said something
bought a medium online and got something that would fit a literal toddler
my dryer ate my favorite hoodie and turned it into a crop top for a chihuahua
spent 4 hours planning tomorrows outfit at 2am and then wore sweatpants anyway
fashion influencers be like 'this is my everyday look' wearing $3000 worth of clothes to get coffee
found a gucci belt at goodwill for $2 and i dont even care if its fake i feel powerful
my shoe collection takes up more space than my actual living area and i regret nothing
dress code says business casual but my boss showed up in cargo shorts so whats the truth
clearance rack at target is my therapy and my therapist cant judge me
someone at work noticed i wore this exact outfit last tuesday and i have never felt more attacked
ordered a cute dress online and what arrived looked like a haunted pillowcase
my laundry shrank my only good pair of jeans and now theyre capris i guess
thrift store find of the century: vintage leather jacket, $5, smells like 1987 but worth it
planning outfits at 3am like im walking a runway when im actually going to walmart
fashion influencer said this top was 'so flattering' it made me look like a stuffed sausage
i own 47 pairs of shoes and still say i have nothing to wear
wore my shirt inside out for half the day and honestly it looked better that way
the clearance section at marshalls is where i become feral
coworker said 'didnt you wear that yesterday' yes and i'll wear it tomorrow too fight me
ordered a large and received something that could double as a tent for a family of four
shrunk my cashmere sweater in the wash now my cat wears it and looks better than i did
2am me adding $400 of clothes to cart. 8am me wearing the same jeans from 2019
fashion bloggers saying 'affordable finds' and linking a $200 white tee shirt lol ok
the dress code at work is 'professional' but i show up in doc martens every single day
scored a ralph lauren blazer at salvation army for $3.50 and i wont shut up about it
i have a dedicated chair in my room thats just for clothes im too lazy to hang up
ordered shoes in my size and they sent me clown shoes. not exaggerating theyre enormous
my white shirt turned pink in the wash and now i just tell people its fashion
someone complimented my outfit and i cant tell them its literally all from the dollar store
wearing the same black leggings 5 days straight. different top tho so it counts as different
bought 6 shirts on clearance i dont need but they were 70% off so actually i saved money
fashion influencer showed a 'capsule wardrobe' that costs more than my car
my mom shrunk my favorite band tee and our relationship has never recovered
its 2:47am and im reorganizing my entire closet by color instead of sleeping
the online pic showed a sleek blazer. what arrived looks like a trash bag with buttons
i rebel against business casual by wearing increasingly loud socks nobody can stop me
found a $400 jacket at the thrift store for $8 and it became my entire personality
my shoe closet needs its own zipcode at this point and im still buying more
put on the same hoodie for the 11th day. its not dirty its seasoned
clearance rack energy is unmatched. saw two women almost fight over a $4 blouse today
fashion youtuber said 'everyone needs this' its $89 for a plain white tank top no thanks
accidentally wore two different shoes to work and just owned it like it was intentional
my dryer has personally victimized every good piece of clothing i own
planning a weeks worth of outfits at 1am then wearing outfit #1 all five days
ordered a size 10 shoe and got a size 10 for ants apparently
the thrift store near campus has better stuff than the mall and im not even joking
wore a hawaiian shirt to the office on monday to assert dominance over dress code
my closet is 90% black clothes and i still spend 30 min deciding what to wear
influencer said these jeans were 'true to size' i cant breathe in them
found brand new nikes at goodwill for $6 and i peaked in life that day
someone photographed my outfit for a 'what not to wear' blog and honestly fair
shrunk my work pants and had to safety pin them shut for an entire shift
its 3am and im on shein adding things to my cart that will look nothing like the photo
dress code says no jeans but my manager wears jeans every friday make it make sense
i have enough shoes to wear a different pair every day for 2 months help
bought a coat on clearance in july because it was $12 and im a visionary
that influencer 'haul' video made me buy 8 things and i looked good in exactly zero
wore my shirt backwards all day and three people complimented it so whos really wrong here
the washing machine ate one sock again. i have 23 single socks now. starting a museum
thrift store changing rooms have seen me at my most vulnerable and most victorious
online order said 'vintage blue' it arrived looking like a sad grey dishrag
my coworkers think i have a huge wardrobe but its actually just 5 outfits on rotation
bought heels on sale that are a half size too small. i will simply not feel pain
its 2am and im pinning outfit ideas on pinterest for a life i absolutely do not live
fashion disaster: tucked my skirt into my tights and walked through the whole office
clearance section is my love language and i wont apologize for it
influencers really out here pretending they bought that designer bag and didnt get it free
the dryer turned my normal shirt into something only a very buff baby could wear
someone asked if my outfit was 'vintage inspired' no babe its just old
ordered an xs and could fit three of me in it. sizing is a social construct at this point
snuck into work wearing sneakers under my dress pants. nobody looks at feet right
found a cashmere scarf at the bins for 50 cents and i bring it up in every conversation
my 2am outfit planning sessions are more productive than anything i do during the day
wore the same dress to 3 different weddings this year. different accessories tho so its fine
fashion influencer said white after labor day is fine now and my grandma is NOT having it
i keep buying running shoes but the only thing i run from is my problems
clearance rack gave me trust issues. bought a 'great deal' that fell apart after one wash
wore pajama pants to target and saw my boss there. we made eye contact. nobody spoke
the thrift gods blessed me with a perfect vintage denim jacket today i could cry
every fashion guru says invest in basics but my basics cost more than my rent now
shrunk my roommates sweater and replaced it with a thrift store one. she hasnt noticed yet
got dress coded at work for wearing a band tee. its metallica not a crime janet
online shopping at 4am is a dangerous sport and i am an extreme athlete
my shoe rack collapsed under the weight of my collection and i took it as a sign to buy another rack
someone told me my outfit was 'brave' and i still dont know if it was a compliment
found a pristine north face at the thrift for $4 and nobody can ruin this day for me
fashion influencer did a 'dupe' video and the dupes were still $60 each lmao
wore mismatched socks on purpose and started a trend in my office. leadership material
the shrinkage from one hot wash turned my sweater into a very fashionable belly shirt
spent 45 min on an outfit for a zoom call where only my face showed
clearance shopping is a competitive sport and i am an olympian
ordered 'army green' pants online. they are bright lime. in what army
my fashion sense peaked in 2014 and ive been chasing that high ever since
thrift store employees know me by name now and honestly thats fine
rebelling against smart casual by wearing the dumbest casual i can find
woke up at 2am thinking about whether stripes and plaid can coexist. the answer is no
the lie that influencers wear those heels all day is the biggest scam of our generation
bought shoes that were too small because they were the last pair and i have faith in stretching
my laundry pile has achieved sentience and is now choosing outfits for me
wearing my stained hoodie inside out is not lazy its called problem solving
